verdicts.rationale: NPPES confirms a Medical Oncology taxonomy, satisfying the specialty portion of the requirement, but there is no self-report or site data confirming she practices within a designated breast center/program.
verdicts.requirement: The investigator must specialize in breast cancer diagnosis and multi-modality treatment (surgical oncology, medical oncology, or gynecologic oncology with a breast focus) to appropriately manage assignment across endocrine, chemotherapy, and targeted-therapy sub-protocols.
verdicts.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists hematology/oncology, medical oncology, surgical oncology, or gynecologic oncology, AND the physician practices within a designated breast center/program.

verdicts.rationale: PubMed record includes multiple breast cancer biomarker/response-prediction publications, including ctDNA in neoadjuvant endocrine therapy (PELOPS trial), Ki-67/pRb pharmacodynamics in TBCRC 035, and TIL scoring in TBCRC030, directly matching the trial's biomarker-guided induction concept.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should have demonstrated expertise in biomarker-based risk stratification and response prediction in breast cancer (e.g., Ki-67, genomic recurrence scores) consistent with the trial's core scientific aim.
verdicts.satisfies_if: PubMed record includes authored/co-authored publications on breast cancer biomarkers, risk assessment, or therapy response prediction (e.g., Ki-67, recurrence score).

verdicts.rationale: Medicare claims show oncology E/M and hospital-care billing (206 services, 134 beneficiaries) confirming an active oncology practice, but the codes are generic visit codes rather than breast-cancer-specific diagnostic/treatment billing, and no self-report volume data is available to confirm sufficiency for very high per-site enrollment.
verdicts.requirement: The practice must see a high volume of newly diagnosed early-stage invasive breast cancer patients spanning adult and elderly age ranges, sufficient to support the trial's very high per-site enrollment.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Medicare claims show breast cancer diagnostic/treatment billing (biopsy, mastectomy, chemo administration) at or above comparable breast-center benchmarks, OR self-report confirms an annual new breast cancer diagnosis volume adequate for large-scale trial enrollment.

verdicts.rationale: This criterion depends solely on self-report of caseload composition, which is absent from the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: Practice must regularly diagnose and treat female patients with unilateral, primary (non-recurrent, non-metastatic) invasive breast carcinoma at the point of initial treatment decision-making.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms that the majority of the physician's breast cancer caseload consists of newly diagnosed, early-stage, unilateral disease rather than recurrent/metastatic referrals.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report or site-level confirmation of certified multidisciplinary breast center status is present in the dossier, despite trial abstracts mentioning Dana-Farber Cancer Institute affiliation, which is not a qualifying data source here.
verdicts.requirement: Site must function as, or be embedded within, a multidisciplinary certified breast center capable of coordinating surgery, chemotherapy, endocrine therapy, and targeted therapy under a single induction-therapy decision pathway.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site is a named/certified Brustzentrum or equivalent multidisciplinary breast center, confirmed by site listing or self-report describing integrated surgical/medical/radiation oncology services.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report describing a longitudinal tumor registry or 8-year follow-up infrastructure is present in the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: Site must maintain infrastructure for sustained patient follow-up and outcome tracking over an 8-year horizon as required by the primary endpoint.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms an established longitudinal tumor registry/follow-up program capable of tracking breast cancer patients for 8+ years.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report describing tissue/biomarker sample collection or shipping workflows is present in the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: Site must have tissue/biomarker sample collection and shipping workflows to support centralized biomarker assessment used to stratify induction therapy assignment.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms an established pathology workflow for biopsy/tissue processing and shipment to a reference lab for biomarker testing.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report confirming capability to run three or more concurrent treatment arms is present in the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: Site must be able to manage patients across multiple concurrent treatment sub-protocols (endocrine, chemotherapy, targeted therapy) with distinct monitoring pathways within a single trial.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms operational capability to run three or more concurrent treatment arms/sub-studies simultaneously.

verdicts.rationale: ClinicalTrials.gov lists her as an overall official on completed cooperative-group-style adjuvant endocrine/palbociclib breast cancer trials, including a completed HR+/HER2- adjuvant endocrine therapy trial and the PALLAS program, consistent with academic cooperative group sponsorship.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience serving as a site investigator within academic cooperative group breast cancer trials, consistent with sponsorship by an academic study group.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as an investigator/site contact on at least one completed breast cancer trial sponsored by an academic cooperative group in ClinicalTrials.gov.

verdicts.rationale: The dossier lists trial titles and statuses but provides no enrollment counts, so there is no basis to assess large-scale enrollment contribution.
verdicts.requirement: Site should demonstrate a track record of contributing large patient numbers to prior trials, consistent with this trial's extremely high per-site enrollment.
verdicts.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov records show the site/investigator contributing enrollment in the hundreds or more on a prior trial, or self-report confirms comparable historical enrollment capacity.

verdicts.rationale: Open Payments records show multiple research payments tied to breast-cancer-specific therapeutics (giredestrant for ER+/HER2- breast cancer and an ER+ breast cancer RYZ101 trial), indicating active ongoing industry-sponsored breast oncology research engagement.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should show an active pattern of clinical research engagement in breast oncology, evidencing ongoing trial participation beyond routine clinical practice.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research-related payments associated with breast cancer therapeutics tied to the physician or their institution.

narrative: This physician shows strong, well-documented expertise in biomarker-driven breast cancer therapy and clear cooperative-group and industry-sponsored trial experience — the strongest signals in this dossier, directly aligned with ADAPT's core scientific design. The specialty match (medical oncology) is confirmed, but the dossier is silent on nearly all operational-capacity and patient-population criteria (breast center certification, multi-arm management, biomarker logistics, long-term follow-up infrastructure, and caseload composition), all of which depend on self-report that was not collected. Medicare billing data reflects a real but only generically characterized oncology practice, not breast-cancer-specific volume. A short self-reported practice profile describing breast center infrastructure, annual new-diagnosis volume, and follow-up/registry capacity would substantially clarify the weaker areas of this assessment.
